etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "tree") + 日(日) (rì, "sun") = sun obscured by trees -> hidden, dark
etymology.interpretation: The character 杳(yǎo, "hidden") visually represents a tree 木(木) (mù, "tree") with the sun 日(日) (rì, "sun") behind it, suggesting the idea of something being concealed or obscured, hence the meaning 'hidden' or 'dark'.

examples.chinese: 在山的那一边(在山的那一邊)，一切都显得杳然(一切都顯得杳然)。
examples.pinyin: Zài shān de nà yī biān, yīqiè dōu xiǎnde yǎorán.
examples.english: On the other side of the mountain, everything seems hidden.

examples.chinese: 他的踪迹(他的蹤跡)一直杳不可寻(一直杳不可尋)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de zōngjì yīzhí yǎo bùkě xún.
examples.english: His whereabouts have always been hidden without a trace.

examples.chinese: 过去的记忆(過去的記憶)已经变得杳杳无踪(已經變得杳杳無蹤)。
examples.pinyin: Guòqù de jìyì yǐjīng biàn de yǎoyǎo wú zōng.
examples.english: Past memories have become completely hidden.
